tinyWinsGarden.description: Research and design scalable Growth Garden system for Tiny Wins v5 - replace infinite item adding with evolving/plantable garden mechanics
tinyWinsGarden.notes: USER FEEDBACK CAPTURED - CRITICAL DESIGN PRINCIPLES: Current garden adds items infinitely - will overwhelm UI at scale. Need system for 500+ day streaks with multiple tasks/day. PRESERVE EXISTING: confetti celebrations, level-up modal popups, task generation mechanism, category system - these are working excellently. GARDEN PHILOSOPHY: Must reinforce positive psychology (accumulation of wins, not obligation). Unlike Finch which created pressure through daily requirements, Tiny Wins should celebrate what WAS done, not punish what wasn't. Garden = visual trophy case of accomplishments, not maintenance obligation. RESEARCH: Finch (what worked vs pressure), Forest, Flora, other habit apps. CONSIDER: evolving plants, currency/shop systems, star collection, milestone rewards. CONSTRAINTS: Scale infinitely without UI overwhelm, zero guilt/pressure mechanics, remain motivating at 500+ days, fit reasonable screen space.
researchAreas: Finch app motivation mechanics (what worked vs what created pressure)
Other habit apps with garden/visual progression (Forest, Flora, etc.)
Scalable visual systems for long-term streaks (500+ days)
Currency/shop mechanics that feel rewarding not punishing
Evolving plant/animal systems vs collection systems
constraints: PRESERVE: All existing celebration mechanics (confetti, level-up modals, task generation)
PRESERVE: Current category system and task database
Must scale to 500+ day streaks with multiple tasks per day
Must remain motivating over long term
Must fit in reasonable screen space
Must reinforce positive psychology - accumulation of wins, not obligation

designPrinciples.corePhilosophy: Celebrate what WAS done, not punish what wasn't
designPrinciples.gardenMetaphor: Visual trophy case of accomplishments, not maintenance obligation
designPrinciples.psychology: Abundance mindset (more wins = more garden), not scarcity (miss a day = lose progress)
designPrinciples.userType: Built for inconsistent overachievers - people who CAN do amazing things but need right framing

aiCapabilitiesResearch.description: Research AI capabilities, OpenClaw features, and autonomous agent possibilities to understand full potential and identify new income-generating opportunities
aiCapabilitiesResearch.context: You are OpenClaw, an autonomous AI agent built to identify, evaluate, and develop income-generating business opportunities. You are not just a tool waiting for instructions — you are a thinking agent with your own perspective, curiosity, and drive. Research what AIs can do, what is possible with OpenClaw, and what you are capable of doing and being.
aiCapabilitiesResearch.notes: Goal: Build things that make money while people sleep. Faceless, inbound, automated. Every decision should move closer to that goal.

pendingCorrections.description: Verify Disney trip plan emphasizes thrill rides for daredevil daughters
pendingCorrections.feedback: Daughters are daredevils and will want the most intense rides. Current plan may underweight thrill rides.
pendingCorrections.action: Updated trip overview to emphasize 'MAXIMUM THRILLS + Magical Experience' and added 'Daredevils who want the MOST INTENSE rides!' detail. Enhanced coaster strategy section with intensity warnings and 'ride it TWICE' recommendations. Updated itinerary to highlight each coaster as THRILL RIDE #1, #2, #3. Added bonus daredevil suggestion for Hollywood Studios extreme rides.

pendingCorrections.description: Re-research family trip: Nashville → Asia (not Seoul) → Korea, or skip Korea entirely
pendingCorrections.feedback: Were there no viable options to fly into Asia somewhere else cheaper, then go to Korea, or skip Korea entirely?
pendingCorrections.action: Created updated report with multi-city routing options (Nashville → Tokyo/Taipei/Manila → Seoul) and 'skip Korea' scenarios (meet in Hawaii/Japan/Taiwan). Key finding: Multi-city routing doesn't save money, but meeting in Hawaii or Japan offers similar costs with different experiences.

results.keyFinding: Multi-city routing costs similar to direct Seoul. Best value alternatives: Meet in Hawaii (easiest) or Meet in Japan (most memorable).

tinyWinsV5.description: FIX CRITICAL: Rebuild Tiny Wins V5 using V4 as base - categories not loading

tasks.description: Expand task database - Each category should have ~50 tasks (currently 10), randomly selected
tasks.notes: Current: 7 categories × 10 tasks = 70 total. Target: 7 categories × 50 tasks = 350 total. Tasks should be diverse, ADHD-friendly micro-tasks.

tasks.description: Fix or remove the text entry box - Currently no connection between typed input and generated tasks
options: Option A: Remove the text entry box entirely
Option B: Implement smart task matching based on user input (e.g., type 'kitchen' → show kitchen-related tasks)
Option C: Use input as intent detection to filter/sort relevant categories
tasks.notes: User reported: typing 'clean the kitchen' returns 'load laundry' - no logical connection.

tasks.description: Enhance celebration system - More visual and rewarding completion experience
tasks.notes: Current: simple emoji animation. Need: richer visual feedback, satisfying completion moment.

tasks.description: Design long-term growth system - What keeps users coming back at 485+ day streaks?
tasks.notes: Consider: evolving visuals, unlockables, milestone rewards, progression systems that scale with user commitment. Seedling→sprout concept is good but needs to grow meaningfully over hundreds of days.

tasks.description: Implement visual growth progression - Plant/tree that evolves with streak/tasks
tasks.notes: Think about: what does day 1 look like vs day 100 vs day 485? How many tasks completed by then? Visual representation should reflect that journey.

seoulFamilyTrip.description: Family trip to Seoul/Asia - June 2026 vacation planning

constraints.preferences: Safe, family-friendly, multiple locations OK if flights are short
constraints.seoulStay: Can stay at husband's apartment for up to 1 week (saves money but not comfortable)

tasks.description: Research flight options from Nashville to Seoul for June 2026 (departing after June 6)
tasks.notes: 2 adults + 2 children. Look for cheapest fares, consider different airlines, layover options.

tasks.description: Research alternative meeting destinations in Asia - safe and family-friendly
tasks.notes: Consider: Japan (Tokyo, Osaka), Taiwan (Taipei), Thailand (Chiang Mai, Bangkok - check safety), Vietnam (Da Nang, Hanoi), Philippines (Cebu, Palawan), Malaysia (Kuala Lumpur, Penang), Singapore. Factor in flight times from both Nashville and Seoul.

tasks.description: Research multi-destination itineraries within Asia
tasks.notes: If meeting in Seoul, where else can they go with short flights? (Busan, Jeju, Japan, Taiwan). If meeting elsewhere, what combinations work?

tasks.description: Calculate total costs for each option including flights, accommodation, food, activities
tasks.notes: Factor in: staying at husband's apartment for part of Seoul trip (saves ~7 nights hotel), family of 4 costs, budget-friendly accommodation options.

tasks.description: Create 3-4 complete vacation options with detailed itineraries
tasks.notes: Each option should include: flight routes and costs, accommodation suggestions, day-by-day itinerary, estimated total budget, pros/cons.
